title: It's all about choices: Support Pasa's summer raffle
description: GEORGE NEWS - We all want mentally healthy...
bodyText:
GEORGE NEWS - We all want mentally healthy and well-adjusted teenagers as our future leaders. Organisations such as Pasa* (People Against Substance Abuse) are doing their utmost to ensure we have a fighting chance to realise this aspiration.


> With Pasa's Summer Madness fundraising raffle, supporters stand a chance to win one of 27 different prizes valued over R17 000 in total, while supporting a great cause.


For every five tickets purchased, supporters will receive one extra ticket. Tickets cost R50 each and are available at Pasa, Wildman George, New York Corner at Astron Energy York Street, Laerskool Blanco and Anysberg Biltong.

The first prize is a whole lamb; the second prize a Wildman voucher of R2 500 and the third prize, one of two accommodation vouchers. The winning tickets will be drawn on Friday 29 November at Wildman George.


> "Pasa is dedicated to empowering youth to make informed choices," said Freddy Trout, Pasa's executive director and founder. "This raffle helps us continue our vital work."


Businesses or individuals who would like to help sell tickets, or have a voucher or prize to donate, please contact Wayne Windvoël on 071 469 9353 or [email protected]. Follow Pasa on Facebook and Instagram.

For nearly two decades, People Against Substance Abuse (Pasa) has been at the forefront of substance abuse prevention and life skills education in local schools. Over the years they've reached thousands of students, equipping them with essential tools to navigate life's challenges.

‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’
